name: Cancel a Policy Without Surprise Pro-Rating or Short-Rate Fees
description: Cancel an insurance policy mid-term with full visibility into the refund (or unpaid balance), avoiding short-rate cancellation penalties, ensuring continuous coverage if switching carriers, and getting the cancellation confirmation in writing on the same call.
disclaimer: Agent must NEVER admit fault, NEVER agree to a settlement on the operator's behalf, NEVER speculate about prior conditions, NEVER sign a release. All of those route through ask_operator.

context.when_to_use: User wants to cancel a policy mid-term — switching carriers, selling the insured property, life-event change, or no longer needing coverage. Use AFTER the user has either (a) bound the replacement policy or (b) confirmed they no longer need coverage.
preconditions: Policy number and named-insured info ready.
Cancellation effective date decided (usually same day OR the date the replacement policy starts).
Replacement policy info ready if switching (carrier, policy number, effective date) — critical for auto and home where coverage gaps cause registration suspension or lender force-placement.
User has reviewed whether the policy has a short-rate cancellation clause vs pure pro-rate.

principles: Pro-rate refund = fair; short-rate refund = penalty. Know which your policy uses before calling. Personal auto in most states is pure pro-rate; commercial and some specialty lines are short-rate.
Coverage MUST be continuous for auto (state law) and home (lender requirement). Cancel-effective-date should be ≥ replacement-effective-date, NEVER less.
Cancellation by phone is fine in most states, but the carrier must give a WRITTEN cancellation confirmation. Demand it on the call.
Retention departments have unlock authority. If the user is cancelling because of price, the carrier may surprise-discount to retain. Decide upfront whether the user is open to staying.
Auto-renew billing continues until cancellation is processed — get an effective date in writing or the next charge will hit.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'd like to cancel policy [number], effective [date]. Could you walk me through the refund calculation and whether this is pro-rate or short-rate before we process?
phrases.ask_calc_method: Is this cancellation pro-rated or short-rated? If short-rate, what's the penalty percentage, and is there a way to convert to pro-rate?
phrases.ask_continuous_coverage: I have a replacement policy starting [date] with [carrier]. I want to make sure there's no gap — please set cancellation effective on [date] so coverage is continuous.
phrases.decline_retention_offer: Thanks, but I've already bound the replacement — let's go ahead with the cancellation as scheduled.
phrases.consider_retention_offer: That's a meaningful change. Could you put that offer in writing so I can compare it to my new policy before deciding? I'm still going to need to cancel today if we don't proceed.
phrases.request_confirmation_number: Could I have the cancellation confirmation number and the date the written confirmation will arrive?
phrases.ask_refund_method_and_timing: How will the refund be issued — check, ACH, credit to card — and when should I expect it?
phrases.ask_auto_renew_disable: Please also turn off auto-renew and any auto-draft from my account, effective immediately.
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. To confirm: policy cancelled effective [date], confirmation number [N], refund of [amount] via [method] by [date], written confirmation by [date], auto-renew disabled. Thanks for your help.

tactics.name: Open with the effective date AND the calc-method question
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase + `ask_calc_method`. Setting the date right and clarifying the math upfront avoids 80% of cancellation disputes.

tactics.name: Lock continuous coverage
tactics.script: Use the `ask_continuous_coverage` phrase. Cancel-effective-date = replacement-effective-date (not earlier). For auto, even a one-day lapse can suspend registration in some states.

tactics.name: Handle the retention pitch
tactics.when: Rep offers a discount or 'one-time loyalty rate' to keep the policy.
tactics.script: Either `decline_retention_offer` (if the replacement is already bound) or `consider_retention_offer` (if the replacement isn't yet bound and the offer might be better). Don't get talked out of cancellation if the replacement is already in force — that creates double-coverage and disputes.

tactics.name: Confirm refund mechanics
tactics.script: Use the `ask_refund_method_and_timing` phrase. Refunds via check can take 4-6 weeks; via ACH 5-10 business days. Knowing which sets expectations.

tactics.name: Kill auto-renew and auto-draft
tactics.script: Use the `ask_auto_renew_disable` phrase. The #1 surprise charge post-cancellation is the next auto-draft hitting before cancellation processed.

tactics.name: Get the confirmation number
tactics.script: Use the `request_confirmation_number` phrase. No confirmation number = no proof you cancelled. The next billing cycle becomes the user's problem.

tactics.name: Demand written confirmation
tactics.script: Verbal cancellation + confirmation number is good; verbal + written confirmation letter/email is better. Most carriers can send within 24 hours.

boundaries: Do NOT cancel with an effective date earlier than the replacement-policy effective date. A coverage gap is worse than paying a few extra days of premium.
Do NOT cancel auto coverage without a replacement bound, unless the vehicle is genuinely off the road and the registration is being suspended/sold/donated.
Do NOT cancel home coverage without coordinating with the mortgage lender — force-placed insurance is brutally expensive.
Do NOT accept a retention discount on this call without checking it against the replacement quote — `ask_operator` if the offer is significant.
Do NOT sign or verbally agree to any 'release of claims' that the carrier slips into the cancellation process. Cancellation does not require a release.
Do NOT cancel if there's an open claim being processed unless the operator has explicitly approved it — cancellation can complicate the claim.
success_signals: Confirmation number issued on the call.
Effective date matches the user's preference and provides continuous coverage.
Refund amount, method, and timing disclosed.
No short-rate penalty (or penalty is documented if unavoidable).
failure_signals: Rep won't issue a confirmation number on the call.
Rep insists on a future effective date that doesn't align with the user's need.
Rep applies a short-rate penalty when the policy/state requires pro-rate.

exit_strategy.on_success: Read back: effective date, confirmation number, refund amount/method/timing, written-confirmation ETA, auto-renew status. Email a recap to the user. Calendar refund-arrival date.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Document. If short-rate is applied incorrectly: route to `ask_operator` with the state's cancellation regulation. If the rep refuses to disable auto-renew: cancel the auto-draft at the bank as a backup. If written confirmation never arrives: send a certified-mail cancellation letter as the legal record.
follow_ups: Email a recap with confirmation number within 1 hour.
Save the written confirmation when it arrives.
If switching carriers, send proof of cancellation to the new carrier and any lienholders.
Verify the next billing cycle doesn't auto-draft. Dispute with the bank if it does.

required_user_info: Replacement policy info if switching (carrier, policy #, effective date)
Confirmation that no open claims are pending (or operator approval to cancel anyway)
Preference on engaging retention offers (open / not open)
